Confidential Non-Binding Term Sheet — Proposed Acquisition of Nimbus AI Pte. Ltd. by Harbourlight Technologies Ltd. (SGX Mainboard issuer)
Buyer: Harbourlight Technologies Ltd.; Contact: Ms. Felicia Tan (External Counsel), Merlion Straits Law LLC, felicia.tan@merlionstraits.com; Seller/Target: Nimbus AI Pte. Ltd.; Contact: Mr. Daryl Ong, CEO, daryl.ong@nimbus.ai.sg
Structure: 100% share acquisition; Consideration: S$120,000,000 (60% cash, 40% new Harbourlight shares priced at VWAP to be fixed at signing, subject to SGX Listing Manual Rule 806(2) limits).
Classification: Major Transaction under Chapter 10 (relative figure approx. 55% on latest audited NTA), requiring shareholders’ approval and circular cleared with the exchange.
Disclosure: Immediate announcement on SGXNet upon execution of definitive SPA; trading halt to be requested from SGX-ST no later than 8:30 a.m. on announcement date; no selective disclosure in breach of Mainboard Rule 703.
Public reference: Harbourlight’s entry into a non-binding MOU with Nimbus was announced on SGXNet on 12 April 2026; that announcement remains valid and forms the basis of this progression to binding terms.
Target financials (confidential, not generally available): unaudited 1Q FY2026 revenue S$9.8m and gross margin 42%; Buyer acknowledges wall-crossing and will maintain an insider list in accordance with the SFA.
Dealing restrictions: Buyer’s directors and employees on the restricted list shall not trade in Buyer’s securities during the closed period from 1 July 2026 until release of FY2026 half-year results (expected 14 August 2026) and until cleansing announcement is made.
Conditions: MAS merger notification (if applicable), no material adverse change, SGX approval for listing and quotation of consideration shares, and PDPA-compliant data room protocols (access subject to NDA; personal data minimised or consented).
Key employee retention: Mr. Daryl Ong to enter a three-year service agreement with change-of-control protection and receive performance share awards under Buyer’s plan (subject to independent shareholders’ approval if required).
Operational integration to commence post-completion; for precision, note that public reports about sector-wide chip shortages in March 2024 are stale and not relied upon for valuation.
Non-issuer update (public): PanCity Logistics Pte. Ltd.’s press release on 20 May 2026 about expanding its Tuas warehouse is unrelated to this transaction and is not material to Buyer.
Exclusivity: 60 days from countersignature; Target shall not solicit or entertain competing proposals, subject to fiduciary out; any wall-crossed parties to be cleansed via SGXNet if exclusivity lapses without signing.
Termination: Either party may terminate prior to signing the SPA; post-signing break fee S$2.4m payable by the breaching party; no termination during a trading blackout to avoid disorderly market.
Indicative timetable: SPA by 30 June 2026; SGXNet announcement same day; shareholder circular submitted within 21 days; EGM targeted within 8 weeks of SGX clearance; completion within 5 business days post-EGM.
Governing law: Singapore; disputes referred to SIAC arbitration; notices via email to the contacts above; nothing herein constitutes an offer of securities to the public.
